REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS-DEFENDANT ZHIJUN WU'S MOTION TO TAX OR STRIKE COSTS IS OFF CALENDAR. MOTION FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1110. THE BODY OF THE NOTICE OF MOTION APPEARS TO BE FOR A "SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE," THAT IS NOT THE MOTION BEFORE THE COURT PER THE MOTION AND THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. THE MOTION IS ADDITIONALLY MOOT. THE UNTIMELY MEMORANDUM OF COSTS FILED BEFORE THE REMITTITUR IS STRICKEN ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. THE REMITTITUR ISSUED ON MAY 30, 2023 WAS FILED ON JUNE 6, 2023. CRC 8.278, FURTHERMORE, IT HAS COME TO THE COURT'S ATTENTION THAT THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY HEARD AND RULED ON A JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE COURT'S ORDER ASSOCIATED WITH THE HEARING IS HEREBY VACATED AS NULL AND VOID. NO REMITTITUR ON FILE AT THE TIME OF THE HEARING. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF ZHIJUN WU NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOITON TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT ZHIJUN WU IS GRANTED. THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON JANUARY 17, 2024, BASED ON THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED ON APRIL 20, 2023, IS VOID. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT WAS FILED IN RESPONSE TO THE COURT'S RULING ON MARCH 23, 2023 GRANTING CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. ON JUNE 16, 2023, THE COURT VACATED ITS MARCH 23, 2023 RULING AND ORDER ASSOCIATED WITH THE MOTION FOR JUDGEMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS NULL AND VOID ON THE GROUND THAT NO REMITTITUR WAS ON FILE AT THE TIME OF THE HEARING. ACCORDINGLY, THE OPERATIVE CROSS-COMPLAINT IN THIS MATTER REVERTS TO THE INITIAL CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED ON DECEMBER 28, 2021 AND ANSWERED BY CROSS-DEFENDANT ON JANUARY 5, 2022. THE FRIST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED ON APRIL 20, 2023 IS HEREBY STRICKEN. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

PLAINTIFF ZHIJUN WU NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOITON TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT ZHIJUN WU is GRANTED. The Default entered on January 17, 2024, based on the First Amended Cross-complaint filed on April 20, 2023, is void. Accordingly, the operative cross-complaint in this matter reverts to the initial Cross-complaint filed on December 28, 2021 and answered by cross-defendant on January 5, 2022. The Frist Amended Cross-complaint filed on April 20, 2023 is hereby stricken.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom:
Jingyi Guo, Esq., 650-475-6289, appearing for Jingyi Guo,
Sze-Hang Lo, Esq., 510-788-0480, appearing for Jianbo Zhong,

ARGUED. Ms. Guo reported that she contested the tentative ruling. The Court stated that the issue is with the proof of service, that psychologically the proof of service is an afterthought for most attorneys, that the proof is the first item the clerks will look for, that without a valid proof of service the submission will not move forward as it presents as ex parte communication. Mr. Guo argued that she filed an amended proof of service. The Court informed her that two days needs to be added for email service and that it her amended proof is defective. Ms. Guo requested that this matter be continued. Mr. Lo stated that he would need time to look at the motion.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ZHIJUN WU MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint is continued to August 30, 2022 at 9:30am in Department 501. Parties waive notice. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not Reported. (D501)
